task_completion.description: Load the official ArchiMate shape library in draw.io
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io interface is visible with the 'ArchiMate' shape library expanded or visible in the left-hand panel.
The canvas contains ArchiMate shapes (typically yellow) labeled 'Customer', 'Submit Claim', and 'Damage Report'.
The canvas contains ArchiMate shapes (typically blue) labeled 'Claims Intake Service', 'Policy Administration System', and 'Document Management System'.
The canvas contains ArchiMate shapes (typically green) labeled 'Mainframe', 'DB2 Database', and 'Claim PDF'.

task_completion.description: Connect the elements with appropriate relationships
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are multiple connecting lines (at least 9) between the shapes, featuring different ArchiMate relationship styles (e.g., solid lines, dashed lines, various arrowheads).

task_completion.description: Save the editable diagram as claims_architecture.drawio
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io window title shows 'claims_architecture.drawio' or a file manager shows this file located on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/).

task_completion.description: Export the diagram as a PNG image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager shows 'claims_architecture.png' located on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/), or an image viewer displays the exported PNG.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to build the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the draw.io canvas, dragging shapes, and using menus, rather than generating the .drawio XML file via terminal scripts.

integrity.description: The agent used official ArchiMate shapes rather than basic geometric shapes
integrity.visual_evidence: The shapes on the canvas have the specific ArchiMate layer/element type icons in their top-right corners, not just plain colored rectangles.

integrity.description: The agent exported the PNG using the application's export function
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using 'File -> Export as -> PNG' or the resulting PNG file does not contain draw.io UI elements (like gridlines or selection boxes) that would indicate a simple screen capture.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exactly 9 elements across 3 layers: Business ('Customer', 'Submit Claim', 'Damage Report'), Application ('Claims Intake Service', 'Policy Administration System', 'Document Management System'), and Technology ('Mainframe', 'DB2 Database', 'Claim PDF'). There must be at least 9 relationship connections between these elements. The draw.io application uses ArchiMate 3.0 shapes (which fulfills the 3.1 requirement). The final outputs must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Desktop/claims_architecture.drawio and /home/ga/Desktop/claims_architecture.png.
